g.text: Fork Following Event Sequence — ptrace Multi-Process Bootstrap
⑤ TRACER REGISTERS CHILD + SETS OPTIONS
⑧ STEADY STATE — INTERLEAVED EVENT LOOP
PTRACE_EVENT stop does NOT flip in_syscall toggle — check (status>>16)&0xff BEFORE checking SIGTRAP|0x80
PTRACE_GETEVENTMSG must be called BEFORE resuming parent — message is overwritten on next event
Child initial SIGSTOP must be swallowed (not re-injected) — it is synthetic from kernel auto-attach
PTRACE_SETOPTIONS on child must be set BEFORE ptrace(PTRACE_SYSCALL, child_pid) — race-free bootstrap
KERNEL | saves regs to task_struct; TIF_SYSCALL_TRACE set
STOP | entry stop delivered → PID 1234 TASK_TRACED
ALLOC | task_struct for PID 1235 (~4KB kernel alloc)
COW | copy page tables, mark read-only (copy-on-write)
PTRACE | PTRACE_O_TRACEFORK flag detected on parent
ATTACH | set PT_PTRACED on child task_struct
SIGNAL | enqueue SIGSTOP to PID 1235 signal queue
STOP | PID 1235 enters TASK_TRACED before any instruction
EVENT | set ptrace_message = 1235 in PID 1234 task_struct
NOTIFY | wake tracer wait queue with PTRACE_EVENT_FORK stop
NOTE | toggle NOT flipped — this is not a syscall stop
REGS | identical copy of parent's registers at fork()
MEMORY | COW page table — physical pages shared
WAITING | paused at kernel/fork.c: copy_process() return
NO CODE | zero instructions of new program have run
PTRACE | PT_PTRACED set, tracer PID = tracer process
PID 1234 — fork() Syscall Exit (Returns Child PID)
CLASSIFY | sig == SIGSTOP → initial attach stop
ACTION | swallow — do NOT re-inject SIGSTOP
REASON | synthetic stop from kernel ptrace attach
NEXT | child runs until first real syscall entry
waitpid(-1) Loop — Events from Both PIDs
LOOP | waitpid(-1, &status, 0) blocks on BOTH pids
EVENT A | pid=1234 → WSTOPSIG=(SIGTRAP|0x80) → syscall stop
EVENT B | pid=1235 → WSTOPSIG=(SIGTRAP|0x80) → syscall stop
NOTE | outbuf per-PID prevents line interleaving
